# Ariadne

> Ariadne is a local-first multilingual memory server for Codex, Claude Code, and any MCP client.

## Core facts

- Project site: https://mclaut.github.io/ariadne/
- Source and documentation: https://github.com/mclaut/ariadne
- Latest release: https://github.com/mclaut/ariadne/releases/latest
- License: MIT
- Platforms: Windows x64, macOS Intel and Apple Silicon, Linux x64 and ARM64
- Runtime: Go MCP server, Qdrant vector server, Ollama bge-m3 embeddings
- Retrieval: exact lookup by ID or hybrid dense plus BM25 sparse search, with wing, room, and collection scopes
- MCP tools: memory_save, memory_recall, memory_delete, memory_move
- Privacy: local endpoints by default; Qdrant binds to 127.0.0.1
- Durable capture: decisions, gotchas, releases, deployments, and verified reports are saved immediately without waiting for SessionEnd
- Token efficiency: confirmed savings, recall overhead, and signed net are measured locally without storing content

## Install

Windows uses install.ps1. Linux and macOS use install.sh. Both are available in the repository root.

## Current release

v0.7.0 adds exact memory retrieval by ID, room-scoped semantic recall, proactive immediate saving of durable decisions and completed reports, and honest token accounting split into confirmed savings, recall overhead, and signed net. v0.6.0 added automatic daily diary distillation with backup-first replacement.
